ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT INDUSTRIAL GLOVES CO.'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO STAY BASED ON FORUM NON CONVENIENS. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT FLORIDA IS A SUITABLE ALTERNATIVE FORUM BECAUSE IT DID NOT SHOW THAT ALL REMAINING DEFENDANTS ARE SUBJECT TO JURISDICTION THERE. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILED TO SHOW THAT CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM CONSIDERING THE BALANCE OF PUBLIC AND PRIVATE INTERESTS. THE ONLY ALLEGED EXPOSURE DISCUSSED BY THE PARTIES OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT DID NOT SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF IS EVEN ALLEGING EXPOSURE IN FLORIDA. FURTHER, AT THIS LATE STAGE OF THE CASE, THE BALANCE OF THE RELEVANT CONSIDERATIONS INCLUDING ALL THE WORK THAT HAS BEEN DONE BY THE PARTIES IN RELIANCE ON THIS CASE BEING TRIED IN SAN FRANCISCO AND AVOIDANCE OF ADDITIONAL COSTS AND DELAY ASSOCIATED WITH THE FILING AND PROSECUTION OF A NEW CASE IN FLORIDAWEIGH STRONGLY IN FAVOR OF RETAINING THE CASE IN THIS FORUM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Jul-20-2011 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION To Stay/Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Mar-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Beth C. Hopwood, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD LLP (510-590-9521), appearing for defendant / moving party Industrial Gloves Company
Oren Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties W. Stanley Burroughs, et al.

RULING:
Argued and tentative ruling adopted. Defendant Industrial Gloves Co.'s motion to dismiss and alternative motion to stay based on forum non conveniens are denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Florida is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all remaining defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. Defendant also failed to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum considering the balance of public and private interests. The only alleged exposure discussed by the parties occurred in California. Defendant did not show that plaintiff is even alleging exposure in Florida. Further, at this late stage of the case, the balance of the relevant considerations ' including all the work that has been done by the parties in reliance on this case being tried in San Francisco and avoidance of additional costs and delay associated with the filing and prosecution of a new case in Florida ' weigh strongly in favor of retaining the case in this forum. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
